skill.name: firecrawl-build-scrape
skill.description: Integrate Firecrawl `/scrape` into product code for single-page extraction. Use when an app already has a URL and needs markdown, HTML, links, screenshots, metadata, or structured page output. Prefer this skill over broader crawl patterns when the feature is page-level.
skill.summary: Guide page-level Firecrawl /scrape integrations for known URLs.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution, path-traversal, and weak-cryptography findings are false positives from Markdown inline code, relative links, and descriptive text. The skill has legitimate medium risk because it requires a Firecrawl API key and guides users toward hosted scraping requests that may transmit URLs and page data.

medium_findings.title: Hosted Scraping Requires External Network and API Key
medium_findings.description: The skill declares FIRECRAWL_API_KEY and FIRECRAWL_API_URL inputs, then directs users to Firecrawl implementation documentation. This is expected behavior, but users must protect credentials and understand that URLs and page content may be sent to Firecrawl or a configured endpoint.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key and hosted request language are explicit in the skill metadata and docs references. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but it creates a real credential and data-sharing consideration.

low_findings.title: Command Execution Detections Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The detected backticks are Markdown inline code references to Firecrawl terms such as /scrape and onlyMainContent. No evidence found of shell execution, Ruby code, or user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown prose, not executable source code. There is no command runner, shell invocation, or script file in the scanned content.

low_findings.title: Relative Link Detections Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The ../ sequences appear inside Markdown links to related Firecrawl skills. No evidence found of file reads, file writes, path joins, or user-controlled filesystem access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path traversal strings are only relative Markdown links. The skill contains no code path that could dereference those links as filesystem operations.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Point to Firecrawl Documentation
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs reference the Firecrawl homepage, source repository, and language-specific documentation. No evidence found of hidden endpoints, unrelated domains, or exfiltration targets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All cited URLs use Firecrawl-owned documentation or source locations that match the skill purpose. They are visible references, not code that transmits data.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Lack Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: The cited lines contain descriptions of scraping, hosted requests, and self-hosted deployments. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text has no cryptographic algorithm names or security-sensitive hashing behavior. The static result appears to be a substring or heuristic false positive.

low_findings.title: Dangerous Combination Heuristics Are Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The critical and high heuristic findings combine static signals for code execution, network, credentials, and filesystem access. The underlying command and filesystem signals are Markdown false positives, and no evidence found of obfuscation or data exfiltration intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full scanned file is a Markdown skill guide with metadata and links. The only confirmed risks are expected Firecrawl API key and network usage.

content.value_statement: Teams often need clean content from one known web page without building a crawler. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to integrate Firecrawl /scrape for focused extraction.
actual_capabilities: Guides integration of Firecrawl /scrape for one known URL.
Recommends markdown output as the default extraction format.
Explains when to request HTML, links, screenshots, metadata, or structured page data.
Defines when to use onlyMainContent for article-like pages.
Routes users to language-specific Firecrawl documentation.
limitations: It does not discover URLs before scraping.
It does not handle multi-step browsing or form interactions.
It requires a Firecrawl API key or compatible self-hosted endpoint.
It provides guidance, not a complete application scaffold.

use_cases.title: Prepare Known URLs for Retrieval
use_cases.description: Extract clean markdown from approved pages before indexing or summarization.

use_cases.title: Enrich Product Records
use_cases.description: Pull page content, metadata, and links from company or product pages.

use_cases.title: Monitor Key Pages
use_cases.description: Scrape pricing, changelog, or documentation pages for later comparison.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic Scraping
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner integration for one known page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add Firecrawl /scrape for a single known URL. Return markdown and explain the required environment variables.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Main Article Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Integrate Firecrawl /scrape for an article page. Use onlyMainContent and return markdown suitable for summarization.

prompt_templates.title: Capture Rich Page Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Firecrawl /scrape for a product page. Include markdown, links, metadata, and screenshot guidance only if the consumer needs them.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Scrape Contract
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a narrow Firecrawl /scrape integration for known URLs. Define inputs, output fields, error handling, and when to escalate to search or interact.

output_examples.input: Scrape a known documentation page for a support knowledge base.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan that returns clean markdown, stores source metadata, and keeps navigation noise out of indexed content.

output_examples.input: Extract a vendor pricing page for change monitoring.
output_examples.output: A page-level scrape design that captures markdown and metadata, with guidance for scheduled comparison outside the skill.

output_examples.input: Add scraping to a product enrichment workflow.
output_examples.output: A focused integration outline that accepts one URL, requests only needed formats, and sends results to downstream enrichment logic.

best_practices: Start with markdown output before requesting heavier formats.
Use onlyMainContent when page layout adds irrelevant navigation text.
Keep the integration scoped to one URL and one extraction contract.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to discover URLs from search queries.
Do not request screenshots or HTML unless a downstream consumer needs them.
Do not use scrape for pages that require clicks, typing, or multi-step navigation.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps integrate Firecrawl /scrape when an application already has one URL to process.

faq.question: Does it crawl entire websites?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on one page and recommends other Firecrawl skills for search or interaction flows.

faq.question: What output should I request first?
faq.answer: Start with markdown unless your application specifically needs HTML, links, screenshots, metadata, or structured data.

faq.question: Does it require credentials?
faq.answer: Yes. Hosted Firecrawl requests require FIRECRAWL_API_KEY, and self-hosted deployments may use FIRECRAWL_API_URL.

faq.question: Can it remove page noise?
faq.answer: It recommends onlyMainContent for article-like pages where navigation and layout content add noise.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk notice because it uses an external scraping service and API credentials.
